meta.note: subscription auth; codex reports tokens, not USD

A.reason: It sharply renders a person relaying words from a dead person to a living person.

B.reason: It identifies the medium as uniquely able to hear and relay a dead person's speech to living people.

C.missing: It lacks a link showing that the words told to people are messages received from the dead person; it carries only a belief in hearing the dead and generic speech.
C.reason: It captures purported hearing of the dead and speaking to people, but not their communicative intermediation.

D.missing: It omits both the living-dead relation and intermediary role, carrying only thinking and saying something to another person.
D.reason: The generic thinker-speaker description fits innumerable unrelated people.

E.reason: It renders the believed transmission of the same message from a dead person through someone to a living person.
